findings: Live verification confirms the identity and taxonomy: Tufted Titmouse (Baeolophus bicolor), family Paridae.
The stated length (5.5–6.3 in / 14–16 cm), wingspan (7.9–10.2 in / 20–26 cm), and weight (0.6–0.9 oz / 18–26 g) match All About Birds.
The habitat and behavior claims are supported: woodland/park/orchard habitats; active, acrobatic foraging among twigs; and carrying seeds to a perch to open them.
All listed field marks are supported and shown: gray crest and upperparts, pale face/underparts, large dark eye, short stout dark bill, black forehead patch above the bill, and peachy/rusty flanks.

The bird has one head, one beak, two legs, one tail, and no extra anatomical structures. One wing is visible in side view; the second is naturally occluded. Proportions are slightly stylized, especially the very tall crest and long-looking tail, but remain recognizable and non-material.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

The printed plate text contains no material factual errors. The adjacent 0–15 cm ruler is decorative/ambiguous rather than an explicitly stated specimen scale; if intended as a true scale, the illustrated bird does not visibly span its stated 14–16 cm body length.

verification_sources.title: Tufted Titmouse Identification — All About Birds
